notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.

errorBoundary.title: This page didn't load
errorBoundary.body: Something went wrong on our end. You can try refreshing or head back home.

auth.checkEmail: Check your email to confirm your account, then sign in.

protected.title: Sign in required
protected.body: You need to be signed in to view this page.

artistGate.pendingBody: Your artist account {{name}} is awaiting approval from an administrator. You'll be able to submit music as soon as it's approved — we'll be in touch.
artistGate.applyBody: To submit music you need an artist account. Fill in your details and apply — an administrator will approve the account before you can start submitting releases.
artistGate.rejectedNote: Your previous application was rejected. Reason: {{reason}}
artistGate.bioHint: Tell us about your music, influences, where you're from.
artistGate.applicationSent: Application sent — we'll get back to you.
artistGate.pendingBodySuffix: is awaiting approval from an administrator. You'll be able to submit music as soon as it's approved — we'll be in touch.
artistGate.bioPlaceholder: Tell us briefly about you/the act as an artist.
artistGate.applicationSentBody: Thanks! Your artist account application has been submitted and is being reviewed by an administrator. As soon as it's approved you'll be able to submit music.

settings.languageHint: Used for emails and in-app notifications sent to you.

landing.heroBody: Discover approved music and podcasts, or submit your own work for review.

about.title: About Catalogus Musicus
about.intro1Mid: is the central music catalog within the
about.intro1Suffix: ecosystem — created to organize, preserve and distribute music, metadata and creative works across multiple platforms, projects and audiences.
about.intro2: The catalog is designed as a long-term foundation for:
about.intro3: Rather than treating music as isolated uploads, the platform is built around relationships between:
about.intro4: This allows the catalog to maintain structure, history and consistency across an evolving media landscape.

mr.heading: Media Rosenqvist
mr.p1: Media Rosenqvist is the overarching creative and technical platform behind the ecosystem.
mr.p3: The purpose is to build an independent and sustainable framework for modern media creation and management.
mr.p5: The goal is not simply to publish content, but to build systems that preserve and connect creative works over time.

cpr.heading: Crystal Pier Records
cpr.p1: Crystal Pier Records is the label and release-oriented part of the ecosystem.
cpr.p3: The philosophy is artist-first and independent.
cpr.p4: Rather than chasing short-term trends, the focus is on:
cpr.p5: Crystal Pier Records handles the master release side of the ecosystem and acts as one of the primary origins of music entering the catalog.

gmp.heading: Guerilla Minstrel Publishing
gmp.p1: Guerilla Minstrel Publishing manages the publishing and rights-related side of the ecosystem.
gmp.p3: The publishing structure exists separately from the label structure in order to properly support the relationship between:
gmp.p4: As the catalog evolves, publishing data will become increasingly important for:

ru.heading: Radio Uppsala
ru.p1: Radio Uppsala serves as one of the living recipients of the catalog.
ru.p2: It is not only a digital radio station, but also:
ru.p3: Music from the catalog may be used within:
ru.p5: Radio Uppsala represents the public-facing and audience-oriented side of the ecosystem.

eco.heading: How the Ecosystem Connects
eco.p1: The ecosystem is designed around interconnected but independent components.
eco.p2: Each part has its own role, while still contributing to the same long-term structure.

meta.heading: Metadata & Catalog Philosophy
meta.p1: The catalog is built around structured relationships and metadata integrity.
meta.p3: The system is intentionally designed to support:
meta.p4: A song may exist on several releases while still maintaining accurate relationships to:

tech.heading: Technology & Infrastructure
tech.p1: Catalogus Musicus is being built as a modern media platform rather than a traditional static database.
tech.p3: The platform is intended to support:

vision.heading: Vision
vision.p1: We believe music should be treated as long-term cultural infrastructure — not disposable uploads.
vision.p2: The purpose of the ecosystem is to create:
list1: preservation of artistic identity over time
vision.p3: This is not only a catalog.
vision.p4: It is the foundation of an independent media ecosystem built around music, storytelling, publishing and digital broadcasting.

catalog.title: Catalog
catalog.subtitle: Approved music and podcasts from the community.
catalog.noApprovedBody: Once submissions are approved they'll appear here.

mySubmissions.emptyBody: You don't have any artists yet.
mySubmissions.title: Mine
mySubmissions.subtitleAdmin: All artists in the catalog. Select one to see albums and tracks.
mySubmissions.subtitleUser: Your artists. Select one to manage albums and tracks.
mySubmissions.demoBody: Submitted music is saved to the Media Rosenqvist Catalog and sent to Radio Uppsala for review. Distribution to Spotify, Apple Music etc. is not active.

upload.title: Title
upload.podcastNoticeBody: Upload the episode audio and artwork — we'll review and publish in the catalog.

stepsLabel.tracksDescription: Add all tracks for the release. Drag-and-drop or pick files.
stepsLabel.detailsDescription: The basics of your release. Everything is saved to the Media Rosenqvist Catalog and is editable later.
stepsLabel.platformsDescription: Pick where the release would be distributed. In demo mode distribution is not active.
stepsLabel.rightsDescription: Confirm you have the right to distribute this release.
stepsLabel.reviewDescription: Check your details before submitting for review.

wizard.label: Label
wizard.errorTracksUploading: {{count}} track(s) still uploading or in error.
wizard.errorRights: All rights & ownership boxes must be checked.
wizard.addAtLeastOne: Add at least one track to continue.
